records.ety: [OE. tretis, OF. treitis, traitis, well made. See Treat.]

defs: A written composition on a particular subject, in which its principles are discussed or explained; a tract.
quotes: He published a treatise in which he maintained that a marriage between a member of the Church of England and a dissenter was a nullity.
notes: ☞ A treatise implies more form and method than an essay, but may fall short of the fullness and completeness of a systematic exposition.
